text: The lighthouse had been dark for eleven years when Mara finally climbed its stairs again. She had told herself the whole drive up the coast that she was only coming to sell the property, that the salt air and the sound of the gulls meant nothing to her anymore, that grief had a shelf life and hers had long expired. But standing now at the base of the iron staircase with her hand on the cold railing, listening to the wind push against the old glass far above her, she understood that some returns are not choices so much as debts, and she had been carrying this one quietly in her chest for longer than she cared to admit.
